title: Standard paths + baseline gate
detail: Record host project's setup/start/test/verify commands in .bee/config.json; inject into session preamble; AGENTS startup runs baseline verify before claiming cells (broken baseline = fix-first tiny cell); session finish requires green verify. Unlocks 08 item 4 (verify-all).
predicted_impact: Fresh sessions stop re-deriving how to run/verify the host repo; regressions caught at session boundaries instead of cap time only

title: Five-layer failure attribution on friction
detail: Optional layer field (spec|context|environment|verification|state) on friction backlog entries and trace.friction; grooming reports friction count by layer, largest = bottleneck. Rides with 08's interventions.jsonl.
predicted_impact: Grooming finds the bottleneck layer by cause, not just by topic cluster

title: Review-feedback promotion: executable check first
detail: bee-compounding: recurring review finding/user correction (2+ hits) promotes to an executable check (grep/lint in verify, guard, hook denial) first; critical-patterns.md prose is the fallback. Grooming grades promote-to-check proposals tiny/small.
predicted_impact: Repeated mistakes get mechanically blocked instead of relying on prose being read each session

title: Fresh Session Test in grooming hunt
detail: Five-question audit mapped to artifacts: what is this system->system-overview.md; how organized->reading-map.md; how run/verify->config.json commands; where are we->bee_status. Unanswerable question = backlog item naming the missing artifact.
predicted_impact: Catches system-of-record decay the entropy formula cannot see

title: ERROR/WHY/FIX denial-message contract
detail: State in 07-contracts.md: every refusal from bin/lib or a hook names the rule, the reason, and the next command/action; audit existing strings; add test assertions.
predicted_impact: Refusals teach in one correction instead of causing retry thrash

title: Warning silenced by any recorded command even without verify
detail: bee_status warning and onboarding notice go quiet when e.g. only commands.start is recorded, yet the baseline gate they justify needs commands.verify. Reviewer P3-3.
predicted_impact: A repo can look 'commands: ok' while the baseline gate is inert; consider a softer second-tier warning when commands exist but verify is absent

title: Gate approval recorded late (shape stayed false past Gate 2)
detail: Phase advanced to validating/swarming while approved_gates.shape was still false; fixed manually at claim time. Phase transitions and gate writes are uncoupled.
predicted_impact: A resuming session or bee_status misreads which gates the human actually approved; propose a staleness warning: phase past a gate that is still false (post-review variant already exists)

title: Standard paths + baseline gate
actual_outcome: Adopted in cells harness09-1..5: commands in config.json, preamble injection, status warn, AGENTS baseline gate + session-finish mirror, onboarding capture notice; bee's own repo recorded and green. Reviewer P3 open: any recorded key silences the warning even without verify (backlogged).

title: Five-layer failure attribution on friction
actual_outcome: Adopted in harness09-6: optional layer enum on friction entries + grooming bottleneck-layer report line; first labeled entries already filed this session.

title: Review-feedback promotion: executable check first
actual_outcome: Adopted in harness09-7: check-first rule + decision tree in compounding, lane note in grooming; promotion criteria unchanged.

title: Fresh Session Test in grooming hunt
actual_outcome: Adopted in harness09-8: five-question probe table with run-don't-read rule.

title: ERROR/WHY/FIX denial-message contract
actual_outcome: Adopted in harness09-9: contract in 07-contracts, two claimCell messages strengthened, three shape assertions; audit found the rest already compliant.

title: Verify string with invalid regex passed static review, caught only at runtime
detail: harness10-8 verify used grep -q '[' (unmatched bracket, invalid BRE). Both my authoring and the adversarial checker read it as fine; only the worker running it in Git Bash caught the abort. Lesson: verify strings containing regex/glob metacharacters need a dry-run in the target shell during validation, not just a read.

title: backlog.mjs latches first Status-columned table
detail: readBacklogCounts fixes statusIndex from the first table row containing a status cell; a prior unrelated pipe-table also named Status latches the wrong index and the real backlog counts 0/0/0 (silent under-report). Out-of-contract input (D6 = single scribing-owned table), hence P3. Fix: re-scan header per table block, or require the header row to also carry an ID/Story cell.

title: BACKLOG_STATUSES drift guard is a value-lock, not a cross-file guard
detail: Unlike COMMAND_KEYS (duplicated across onboard_bee.mjs + state.mjs), BACKLOG_STATUSES is single-source in backlog.mjs, so regex-matching its own source against its export guards nothing cross-file. The value-lock half (enum === D6 literal) is useful; the source-regex half is redundant. Fix: keep the value-lock, drop the source-regex block, or assert no other lib defines a rival status literal.

title: datamark guillemet fence is breakable
detail: datamark wraps in «…» but does not strip « or » from content, and newlines survive. A title containing » closes the fence early; the rest renders unfenced. Also unstripped: ~~~ fences, markdown headers, unicode bidi overrides, <|im_start|> sentinels. Shared with the decisions surfacing path — fix must re-verify that caller.

title: foreign digest read is unbounded and foreign title is never capped
detail: mergeDigests validates with realpathSync + lstatSync().isFile() but calls readJson with no size guard; a multi-GB feedback-digest.json OOMs the bee session, contradicting D2b one-dead-repo-never-breaks-the-session. capTitle is applied in buildEntry but not in mergeDigests. Fix: statSync size guard (~5MB) + capTitle on foreign titles.

title: bee_feedback.mjs not vendored into .bee/bin (EXISTS+SUBSTANTIVE, not WIRED)
detail: bee-compounding step 8 invokes .bee/bin/bee_feedback.mjs but the helper lives only in templates/. Onboarding correctly plans copy_helper + copy_lib. warn-never-block degrades it to a warning. Remedy is onboard_bee.mjs --apply, human-approved.

title: requireFlag is dead code in bee_feedback.mjs
detail: Defined at bee_feedback.mjs:49-55, never called; run() reads flags.out directly. Carried over from the bee_capture.mjs template shape.

title: unused imports writeCell and collectFeedback in test_lib.mjs
detail: writeCell (line 42) unused — suite uses its own writeCellFile. collectFeedback (line 68) never called; exercised only via buildDigest. The export itself is legitimate.

title: oversize is an unreachable DROP_REASONS value
detail: DROP_REASONS includes oversize and a drift test pins it, but buildEntry caps titles rather than dropping and mergeDigests does neither. plan.md is self-contradictory: D2 step 3 says cap, step 4 lists oversize as a reason. Pick one.

title: entry sorting uses locale-sensitive localeCompare
detail: Idempotence is proven within one process. localeCompare is locale-sensitive, so a digest regenerated on a machine with a different default locale may reorder unicode titles — and the corpus is partly Vietnamese. Use code-unit comparison or a pinned locale if cross-machine stability matters.

title: SECRET_CONTENT_PATTERNS and INJECTION_PATTERNS are narrow heuristics now load-bearing
detail: Missed secrets: Stripe sk_live_, Google AIza, Slack xoxb-, github_pat_, AWS secret key (only AKIA id matches). Missed injections: disregard everything you were told, developer mode, <|im_start|>system, benign-phrased imperatives. Pre-existing, but the digest now leans on them as its only content scanner.

title: Iron Law ordering has no mechanical proof; RED and GREEN landed in one commit
detail: evolving-4 verify greps for ## RED / ## GREEN — touch would pass it. Git cannot prove ordering either: both landed in commit 4a1d991. The only ordering evidence is the orchestrator observing pressure-tests.md holding ## RED while git status on the skill dir was clean. Iron Law cells must commit RED as its own commit before the skill edit.
predicted_impact: A future Iron Law cell can fabricate RED after the fact and no check will catch it.

title: Orchestrator released a live worker reservations on a false stall signal, causing duplicate dispatch
detail: A stall/killed notification for evolving-4 was trusted; the orchestrator released the worker reservations, reset the claimed cell, and spawned a second worker. The first worker was alive and finished; the second returned [NOOP]. No corruption, but the reservation guard was defeated by the orchestrator itself. Never release another agent reservations or reset a claimed cell on a stall signal alone — verify death by checking for disk progress over an interval.
predicted_impact: Lost update or conflicting commits on the next stall misfire.

title: feedback.mjs contains a NUL byte, so grep treats it as binary and silently matches nothing
detail: sortKey joins fields with a NUL separator (feedback.mjs:604) — a legitimate unambiguous-separator technique. Side effect: grep/rg treat the whole file as binary and print nothing, not even 0 matches. In a repo whose drift guards are grep-over-source, this is a trap; it briefly convinced the orchestrator a landed fix had vanished. Consider U+001F (unit separator) or a JSON.stringify-based key.

title: Same defect class survived three rounds because tests only covered remembered fields
detail: mergeDigests neutralized only title (round 1), then title+layer+source (round 2), then first_seen was still raw via Date.parse leniency (round 3). Each round patched the field that was found. The structural fix (ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC: field -> validator, ENTRY_FIELDS derived from it) plus a table-driven per-field payload test is what actually closed the class. Rule: when a boundary enumerates field NAMES, a field without a declared validator must be a test failure, not a vulnerability.
predicted_impact: Any future allowlist-style boundary in bee will repeat this unless the spec-with-validators shape is the default.

title: Two frozen assertions encoded the defects they were meant to guard
detail: test_lib.mjs:1833 asserted a foreign source survives mergeDigests raw — written by the very cell tasked with building the boundary. The ENTRY_FIELDS source-literal grep pinned the bare-name-array syntax that was itself the defect. Both were caught only because a worker tried to fix the bug and the suite refused; both required a planner unfreeze (decisions c45d0fb3, b8fe5c81). 93 and 104 green assertions proved conformance to a wrong spec, not safety. The frozen-assertion rule MUST keep its stop-and-report escape hatch.
predicted_impact: Without the escape hatch, the first defect written into a test becomes permanent and invisible.

title: The one real corpus is a manual check, not a standing fixture
detail: The suite is fixture-driven; anphabe-gogl (the only repo with real history) is exercised only when the orchestrator remembers to run it by hand. It falsified three plan assumptions (pain constant, layer absent, corroboration inert) and caught the normalizeKind regression that 104 green fixture assertions missed. Pin a scrubbed snapshot as a checked-in fixture and assert stable properties (round-trip entry count, no unknown_type on known-real kinds).
predicted_impact: Slice B inherits a thinner corpus than its design assumes, and the next plan will again be written against documented schema rather than produced data.

title: Grep-based guards need mutation-style negative tests
detail: Two grep guards failed in different ways this feature: one pinned the defective syntax it was meant to protect (ENTRY_FIELDS bare array), and one was blind because a NUL byte made the file read as binary. Neither failure was visible. Every grep-based guard should ship a negative test that reintroduces the violation it claims to catch and asserts the guard fires. Retrofit requires auditing every existing grep guard under .bee/ — the repo has now been bitten twice (BACKLOG_STATUSES, ENTRY_FIELDS).
predicted_impact: A third grep guard silently pins the wrong thing, or silently sees nothing.

title: Iron Law RED must land as its own commit before the skill edit
detail: RED-before-GREEN ordering currently has no mechanical proof: the verify greps for headings that touch would satisfy, and RED+GREEN landed together in commit 4a1d991 so commit order proves nothing. Requiring RED as a separate, earlier commit makes ordering checkable from the commit range — cheap, and it turns a human judgment at Gate 4 into a check.
predicted_impact: A future Iron Law cell fabricates RED after the fact and nothing catches it.

title: localeCompare entry ordering is locale/ICU-dependent — byte-identical only per environment
detail: sortKey(a).localeCompare(sortKey(b)) at feedback.mjs:640-641/800-801/811 depends on the runtime locale; rankClusters tie-break already uses codepoint compare. Real corpus measured identical today (divergent index -1) so latent. Fix: one codepoint comparator across the feature, or pin a locale; touches 20+ merge assertions — its own cell. Source: review-slice-b.md P2-1.

title: Wrapper-only or guillemet-quoted titles may false-collapse to the empty cluster key
detail: normalizeTitle strips «…» to fixed point, so a legitimately guillemet-quoted title is indistinguishable from the datamark wrapper and «» normalizes toward the empty string; two distinct empty-ish titles could false-unify. Assert intended behavior for «» and quoted titles. Source: review-slice-b.md P3-5.

title: rank --json emits the datamark-stripped cluster key to the consuming agent
detail: Merge-acknowledged open P1 (Gate 4, 2026-07-10). Project key out of the CLI rank result, render representative stored wrapped title, add a test asserting key absent from rank --json. Source: review-slice-b.md P1-a.

title: normalizeTitle hand-duplicates datamark cleaning with no coupling test
detail: Merge-acknowledged open P1 (Gate 4, 2026-07-10). Export shared datamarkClean from decisions.mjs, call from both datamark and normalizeTitle, add a coupling test over datamark-relevant payloads. Source: review-slice-b.md P1-b.

title: C0 sweep is scoped to templates/*.mjs — widen to the defect class and fix the NUL in reports/evolving-9.md
detail: The sweep misses committed non-.mjs files; reports/evolving-9.md shipped with a raw NUL (git-binary, grep-invisible) two commits after the sweep landed. One cell: replace the byte with a printable escape, extend the sweep (or a docs-level check) to every committed text file, and add the .bee/bin byte-parity assertion (review P3-1/P3-3). Predicted impact: silent grep/diff blindness on any future report narrating control-byte work.

title: External reviewer dispatch lacks an AGENTS.md-bootstrap scope-out — round-1 self-block is reproducible
detail: bee-reviewing dispatches external cli reviewers with the worker prompt shape whose step 1 is Read AGENTS.md; the BEE block then demands onboarding checks a read-only reviewer cannot perform, so it self-blocks (cost: one rescue round, observed 2026-07-10). One tiny cell: add a read-only scope-out line to the reviewer dispatch instructions in bee-reviewing (SKILL.md or reviewing-reference.md) — this process is read-only, skip the bee-hive onboarding bootstrap. Predicted impact: every external review run burns a rescue round until fixed.

title: Small-lane inline reality check has no derived-constants pass — two P1s reached review in a 0-flag feature
detail: Failure analyst cross-cut: the census verified removed names, not preserved invariants (full-tree coverage, bare tokens, numeric constants derived from roster size). Candidate: add a derived-constants line to the small-lane reality check in bee-planning, or mechanize a root-scoped census helper.
impact: P1-class findings land at review instead of planning on removal/rename features

Note: .bee/bin/lib/fsutil.mjs and state.mjs carry pre-existing uncommitted modifications (present at session start, not from this feature) - likely an in-progress vendored-lib edit broke the export. Telemetry-only failure; close proceeded per warn-never-block.

title: onboard_bee.mjs apply has no downgrade guard — a stale plugin can silently re-vendor older helpers over newer committed ones
detail: Root cause of the 0.1.18-over-0.1.19 regression: apply writes whatever templates the installed plugin carries. Candidate fix: refuse (or warn loudly) when template BEE_VERSION < vendored BEE_VERSION in the target repo.
impact: silent helper regressions that surface later as unrelated crashes (e.g. digest readText)

Harden: READ-ONLY dispatch preamble is now de-facto (used 6x this feature) — mechanize via a read-only sandbox flag in the review-slot command preset and/or a swarming-reference rule; consider dropping --yolo from the sample review command.

title: Worker git --amend swept a concurrent worker's staged files

title: Vacuous-UAT Gate-4 bypass reading of decision 0010 not separately ratified
detail: Merge auto-approved because zero SEE/CALL/RUN UAT items existed (no CONTEXT.md, prose-only feature) and P1=0 — 'every UAT item confirmed' satisfied vacuously. Deliberate extension of 0010 to a case its authors may not have anticipated; decide whether vacuous-pass is standing policy or should require human sign-off while novel.

title: Prose temporal-consistency has no automated check — single-reviewer defense
detail: The step-order/artifact-existence defect class is invisible to marker-grep/diff/suite proof surfaces; only the external reviewer catches it, and round 1 was not exhaustive. Consider a cheap heuristic (grep artifact filenames inside earlier-step bullets of SKILL.md flows) as a verify-line or grooming sweep.

title: Fail-open guarantee of hook log writers is untested
detail: logDispatch/logDeny/logCrash claim fail-open by comment only; no test forces the write to throw (.bee/logs as a file, unwritable dir) and asserts exit code/stderr unchanged. Add a throwing-logs fixture row to test_model_guard.mjs.

title: Claiming a cell does not flip phase out of idle — first source edit hits the write-guard
detail: Plan+cell are data-layer writes; nothing enforces the invariant 'cell claimed => phase != idle' before the first Edit. Consider bee_cells claim setting phase, or a claim-time warning when state.phase is idle.

title: Internal .bee mutations still done via Read+Edit/sed — CLI-ify all state mutations
detail: Agents hand-edit .bee JSON (Read pulls whole file into context, Edit spends output tokens, string-match retries waste more). Every mutation on .bee state should go through a single Bash CLI call (.bee/bin/*), atomic and Read-free. Audit which mutations lack a CLI verb (e.g. cell field updates, state phase flips, worker lifecycle) and add them. Token math: one Read of a 2KB cell is ~500-700 input tokens that then compound as cache-read on every later turn; a CLI mutation is ~50-100 tokens flat. SQLite was considered and rejected: it does not reduce tool calls and loses git-diffability at gates.

title: .bee/workers transients never pruned — 66 stale files / 2.8MB of 3.9MB total
detail: Worker prompt/result/log files from finished cells accumulate (2.8MB of the 3.9MB .bee dir). They add noise to ls/glob/grep sweeps and risk an agent reading a stale *.result.md from a previous feature. Auto-prune on cell cap (or a bee_workers.mjs prune verb run at review/compounding wrap-up), keeping only the active feature's transients.

title: bee-compounding writes backlog entries with kind: but the digest collector reads type: — 17 entries silently dropped
detail: Every backlog line bee-compounding has written since 2026-07-10 uses the field kind; buildDigest reads raw.type for local backlog entries, so all 17 land in dropped[] as unknown_type and never reach ranking. Friction the hive collected is invisible to bee-evolving. Fix both sides: collector accepts kind as alias for type on the local path (one line), and the compounding skill's append template says type. Then migrate the 17 stranded lines.

title: rank's top cluster is 14 foreign entries with empty titles — garbage cluster outranks every real signal
detail: anphabe-gogl's digest contributes 14 entries whose titles survive as empty after revalidation; normalizeTitle('') clusters them all under key '' with frequency 14, rank 14, beating every genuine cluster (max 3). clusterEntries should skip entries whose normalized title is empty (they carry no actionable signal) or rank should exclude the '' cluster.

title: bee_state/bee_backlog swallow unknown optional flags — typo'd flag writes wrong data with exit 0
detail: Reproduced: worker add --teir ceiling => tier null; set --sumary => summary lost. Violates plan matrix row 'unknown verb/flag -> ERROR/WHY/FIX'. Fix: per-verb flag allowlist rejection before any write. bee_state.mjs:44-67 bee_backlog.mjs:54-66. Review P2-1.

title: scribing-run hardcodes phase=compounding — workflow routing baked into a mechanical CLI
detail: Matches recorded worker deviation. Fix: --phase flag on scribing-run, bee-scribing passes the target; CLI keeps only the timestamp stamp. bee_state.mjs:244. Review P2-3 (architecture).

title: set --mode is a free string while every sibling field is enum-validated
detail: Typo'd mode silently skews lane ceremony and bypass eligibility. Fix: validate against LANES (cells.mjs:9) with ERROR/WHY/FIX. bee_state.mjs:123. Review P2-4 (architecture).

title: Deny-precedence in a gated phase untested — the exact branch the guards.mjs comment justifies
detail: test_write_guard covers idle+swarming only; a refactor moving the deny below underAllowedPrefix reopens direct edits during planning/validating unnoticed. Fix: planning-phase fixture row. Review P2-5 (test-coverage).

title: worker add allows duplicate nicknames; update hits first match, remove drops all
detail: 3x corroborated (arch+cq+tc P3 -> P2). Fix: reject dup add with ERROR/WHY/FIX pointing at worker update. bee_state.mjs:165-180. Review P2-6.

title: RMW re-read-before-write ordering (C1) has no regression assertion
detail: bee_state re-reads correctly today; nothing turns red if readState is hoisted. Comment-anchored ordering assertion acceptable. Review P3-1.

title: Boundary values title=200 layer=40 never positively asserted

title: Direct-edit deny false-positives on git add / cp involving state files
detail: Reproduced: git add .bee/state.json denied with a message about hand-editing. Scope deny to genuinely mutating targets (exclude git-index and copy-source args). guards.mjs:44-47,255-293. Review P3-3.

title: Hive rule #11 promises all .bee/*.json(l) but the hook enforces only two files
detail: cells/, decisions.jsonl, reservations.json unguarded. Extend DIRECT_EDIT_DENY or narrow the rule wording. Also reword validation C3: bypass class is any unlisted interpreter/binary (node/python/dd/rsync), not just node -e. Review P3-4 (arch+security merged).

title: Arg-parsing primitives now duplicated across three mutation CLIs
detail: bee_state, bee_backlog, bee_cells each carry parseArgs/requireFlag variants. Extract shared value-flag parser into lib/. Review P3-5 (architecture).

title: detail/feature uncapped at rest; next_action/feature rendered into prompts without datamark
detail: Cap detail<=2000 feature<=100 in bee_backlog add; datamark next_action and feature at inject.mjs:117,228 to match the decisions line. Review P3-6 (security x2 merged).

title: bee_decisions log: require a quote/confirmation field when a decision cites the user
detail: Mechanizes the fabricated-attribution class (decision 3d55b976, Minions nicknames — worker cited a user preference never stated). Smallest shape: --user-quote flag required when rationale/source claims user intent; workers never log user-sourced decisions. Tiny cell candidate.

title: .bee/workers transients never pruned — RESOLVED by workers-prune

Review cost: 4 P1s (suffix stemming, flag swallowing, fail-open workers, snapshot race) — deletion CLIs earn their review.

title: Codex hooks are gated behind [features] hooks = true — without it NO bee hook runs, silently (exit 0, no warning)
detail: Found by spike .spikes/codex-runtime-parity/q3-cmdsubst. ~/.codex/config.toml:73-74 carries [features] hooks = true. Nothing in plan.md/CONTEXT.md/approach.md/docs/specs/hook-runtime.md mentions it; onboarding never sets or checks it; a fresh user will not have it. Falsifies the business rule 'exactly one active bee hook source' — on a clean machine there are ZERO. A healthy install and a silently-dead install are indistinguishable today. Needs: onboarding sets/verifies the flag, install verb reports it, spec records it.

title: Rewriting .codex/hooks.json invalidates the 9 per-entry trusted_hash values — cell 6 as written cannot close the incident on the user's machine
detail: ~/.codex/config.toml:37-63 pins each hook by sha256 (path:event:group:idx). Regenerating the file forces a human re-trust in a fresh Codex thread, but cell codex-parity-6 prohibits touching ~/.codex and names no re-trust checkpoint. Needs an explicit out-of-cell Gate-4 human step: re-trust the 9 rewritten hooks, capture /hooks trusted rows + clean Stop.

title: bee-write-guard false-positive: extracts the shell COMMAND NAME as the write target on compound Bash lines
detail: Live repro during validating: 'rm -rf X; mkdir -p Y' inside the allowed .spikes/ path was denied with: writing "mkdir" is blocked. Also denied writing "$SPIKE";" (unexpanded shell var). The guard's target extraction does not handle compound commands or variable expansion, so it blocks legitimate work in allowed paths. Related to the known 'target-regex parser-differential' advisory. Workaround used: Write tool with literal paths.

title: Gate 3 for codex-parity-6 REVOKED by re-validation — prior approval rested on a false READY WITH CONSTRAINTS report
detail: The earlier validation-codex-repo-fallback.md claimed READY WITH CONSTRAINTS with ASSUMPTIONS: PASS and PROOF SURFACE: PASS. All three false: (a) the cell verify 'node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs' is GREEN today against the broken .codex/hooks.json and covers none of truths 1-4; (b) the repo-target transport string was specified nowhere, so a worker would have invented what gets baked into the live hook config; (c) the hook-process cwd — which git rev-parse --show-toplevel depends on — was never proven. Re-validated 2026-07-12 with plan-checker persona panel + cell reviewer (both opus) plus spike q3-cmdsubst: NOT READY - RETURN TO PLANNING. approved_gates.execution set back to false. No code was written under the stale approval.

title: RETRACTED: the earlier P1 '[features] hooks = true gates all Codex hooks' finding is INVALID
detail: My own spike disproves it and I mis-read it: run 2 SET features.hooks=true and the control hook still did not fire. So the flag was never shown to be the cause — I inferred causation from a config diff. The authoritative Codex Hooks contract (https://learn.chatgpt.com/docs/hooks) states hooks are ENABLED BY DEFAULT and disabled only via features.hooks = false. No onboarding/config-flag work belongs in this slice. The real reason the spike control never fired remains unexplained (most likely: codex exec non-interactive does not run SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit project hooks, or per-entry trusted_hash gating). Supersedes the P1 row 'Codex hooks are gated behind [features] hooks = true'. Durable decision: d91a8398-2d63-426b-a133-341568453200

title: test_hook_contracts.mjs silently ignores unknown CLI flags and exits 0 ALL PASS — any verify naming an unimplemented mode passes green
detail: Verified 2026-07-12: 'node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag' exits 0 and prints ALL PASS. Both replacement cells (6a --catalog-only, 6b --repo-route-only) would therefore have passed verify with zero implementation — reintroducing the exact 'verify green against a broken config' defect that got codex-parity-6 rejected. Fix folded into 6a as a strict-flag prerequisite (unknown flag -> print known modes to stderr, exit non-zero, RED before GREEN). Matches critical-pattern 20260708-harness10 'verify strings are authored, not just read'.

title: bee-planning skill says --phase planning-complete but bee_state rejects it (not in the phase enum)
detail: skills/bee-planning/SKILL.md section 6 step 4 instructs: node .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs set --phase planning-complete --next-action 'Invoke bee-validating.' The CLI rejects it: invalid phase 'planning-complete' — enum is idle, exploring, planning, validating, swarming, reviewing, scribing, compounding, grooming, compounding-complete. Either add planning-complete to the enum or fix the skill to use 'validating'.

title: Post-6a divergence: Codex runs the current guarded wrappers while Claude keeps running the stale pre-adapter vendored ones
detail: S2 residue, named by the Gate-3 plan-checker panel and not carried by either cell. .claude/settings.json wires 10 Claude hooks to .bee/bin/hooks/*, where all 7 vendored wrappers differ from hooks/ source and adapter.mjs is not vendored at all (pre-parity: no hostile-stdin normalization, no apply_patch guard, plain-text Stop). After codex-parity-6a lands, Codex-in-this-repo runs the CURRENT guarded wrappers while Claude-in-this-repo still runs the STALE ones. Owned by the E2 Distribution slice (re-vendor via onboarding). Related to the existing 'repo .bee/bin/hooks stale until re-onboard' item.

title: Skip rows are counted as PASS — any required test row registered skip:true yields ALL PASS exit 0
detail: test_hook_contracts.mjs:626-628 constructs skip rows with pass:true, and :1022 computes failures = results.filter(r => !r.pass). A skipped row therefore prints ALL PASS and exits 0. Live precedent at :620-630 (codex CLI absent). This is a general escape hatch in the suite, not specific to this slice: any future cell can satisfy its verify by registering its new row as skippable. codex-parity-6a now carries a required-row-id manifest (absent or skipped => non-zero) as a local fix, but the suite-wide pattern deserves a standing guard.

title: bee-validating skill says --phase validated but bee_state rejects it (same enum drift as bee-planning's planning-complete)
detail: skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md Gate 3 section instructs: node .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs set --phase validated. CLI rejects it — enum is idle, exploring, planning, validating, swarming, reviewing, scribing, compounding, grooming, compounding-complete. Pairs with the already-filed bee-planning 'planning-complete' drift. Both skills instruct terminal-ish phase names the state machine does not know. Either add validated/planning-complete to the enum or fix both skills.

title: PreToolUse write-guard fails OPEN — agent disables its own guard with 'git config core.bare true'
detail: REPRODUCED end-to-end during review. The repo-target transport exits 0 (=ALLOW for PreToolUse) whenever git rev-parse --show-toplevel fails. The guarded agent can force that from inside the repo: 'git config core.bare true' makes rev-parse exit 128 (verified), $r empty, fail-open arm fires, every subsequent PreToolUse exits 0. The guard does NOT intercept 'git config' (extractBashTargets yields zero path targets, verified guard exit 0), so it is a self-service kill switch for bee's gate/reservation enforcement on Codex, reachable by the untrusted party with no privilege escalation. FIX: fail CLOSED for PreToolUse only — thread event into commandFor(script,target,event) in hooks/catalog.mjs:80 and emit for the write-guard entry: r=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$r" ] && [ -f "$r/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs" ] || { echo 'bee write guard: cannot resolve repo root — refusing write' >&2; exit 2; }. Advisory events (SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/PostToolUse/SubagentStop/PreCompact/Stop) keep exit 0 per R4. Also amend spec R2/B6 (docs/specs/hook-runtime.md:111): a deny-capable checkpoint fails CLOSED when its transport cannot start — 'unknown' is not 'safe' for a deny hook. Regenerating .codex/hooks.json after the renderer fix will re-invalidate trusted_hash (Gate-4 re-trust). New fix cell required before merge.

title: Nested git root -> MODULE_NOT_FOUND -> write guard silently OFF (fail-open proves 'a git root exists', not 'bee hooks live there')
detail: CORROBORATED by two independent reviewers (security P2 + code-quality P2-1) -> promoted to P1. The transport bails only when $r is EMPTY. If the session cwd is inside a DIFFERENT git root (nested clone, vendored repo, submodule, a .spikes/ scratch repo), git rev-parse succeeds, $r is non-empty, the visible fail-open arm is SKIPPED, and 'exec node $r/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs' dies MODULE_NOT_FOUND exit 1. Codex treats exit 1 as a non-blocking error -> write proceeds -> guard silently off. This is the ORIGINAL incident's failure mode verbatim, one layer up, and the route harness cannot see it because buildRouteFixture always copies hooks/*.mjs into the fixture root. SAME FIX as the fail-open P1: replace [ -n $r ] with [ -n $r ] && [ -f $r/hooks/${script} ] in repoCommand (hooks/catalog.mjs:72-78); for the write guard that path denies (exit 2), for advisory hooks it prints the pinned diagnostic and exits 0 (visible). Add a 5th route arm: git-init fixture with NO hooks/ dir. Consider also [ -f $r/.bee/onboarding.json ] as a repo-identity check. Fold into the same fix cell as the fail-open P1.

title: Route harness never evaluates the PreToolUse matcher — matcher 'Edit|Write|..|Grep' does NOT match Codex's apply_patch (pre-existing, not a regression)
detail: The new harness flattens hooks[].command and runs each via bash -lc, capturing group.matcher but NEVER asserting it. The configured PreToolUse matcher is Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash|Read|Glob|Grep (verified identical across pre-fix ref, plugin projection, and codex projection — so NOT introduced by this diff), while the payload sent, the adapter parse, and the tool Codex actually emits is apply_patch. new RegExp('Edit|Write|..|Grep').test('apply_patch') === false (verified). If Codex applies matchers to tool names the way Claude does, the Codex write guard is never invoked for apply_patch and the 895-line harness still prints ALL PASS — same shape as the original bug one layer up. UNPROVEN whether Codex matcher semantics actually exclude it; needs a live probe. FIX (test): add a route row asserting new RegExp(cmd.matcher).test(toolNameFor(cmd.event)) — goes RED today, converting a silent assumption into a decidable finding. Separately investigate whether the codex matcher must include apply_patch.

title: repoCommand hardcodes $r/hooks/ — onboarding CANNOT adopt the renderer 'unchanged'; host repos would reproduce MODULE_NOT_FOUND
detail: hooks/catalog.mjs:76 execs node $r/hooks/${script}. That path exists only in bee's OWN source repo; onboarded host repos vendor wrappers to .bee/bin/hooks/ (onboard_bee.mjs:40-57,990). approach.md:43-44 + catalog.mjs:41-51 claim onboarding adopts this renderer unchanged — false: there is a hidden THIRD axis (wrapper location: source hooks/ vs vendored .bee/bin/hooks/) the 'repo' target conflates. If the Distribution slice calls renderProjection('codex',{target:'repo'}) to emit a host .codex/hooks.json as-is, every host hook dies Cannot find module <root>/hooks/bee-*.mjs. FIX: parameterize wrapper dir — repoCommand({target, wrapperDir='hooks'}), '.bee/bin/hooks' for onboarding. Deferred to Distribution but the 'adoptable unchanged' claim in approach.md/catalog.mjs must be corrected now.

title: This diff widens a Claude<->Codex skew in bee's own repo: Codex now runs current guarded wrappers, Claude still runs stale pre-adapter vendored ones
detail: ARCHITECTURE finding. .claude/settings.json points Claude at .bee/bin/hooks/bee-*.mjs (vendored, pre-apply_patch: 0 apply_patch refs vs 12 in hooks/ source; vendored bee-state-sync doesn't import adapter.mjs). Pre-diff, .codex/hooks.json ALSO targeted the vendored copies (via unset CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR), so both runtimes ran the same wrappers. This diff repoints Codex at current hooks/ source — so in the one repo where safety-foundation matters most (bee editing bee), Codex gets apply_patch deny + structured advisories + --source audit and Claude does NOT. Under Claude here, a direct .bee/state.json apply_patch is not denied while spec B3 + Codex both say it is. Vendored staleness is a pre-existing onboarding gap (cell scoped it OUT, 6a:68) but THIS diff makes the runtimes diverge. FIX: re-vendor via onboard_bee --repo-hooks (or point .claude/settings.json at hooks/ for bee-self); if deferred to Distribution, record as a spec coverage gap.

title: commandWindows omitted + spec Open Gaps does not name Windows/non-POSIX $SHELL — undeclared, not merely deferred
detail: api-contract. commandFor (catalog.mjs:80-83) and renderProjection (:207-211) emit only {type,command,statusMessage}; Codex HookHandlerConfig::Command also carries commandWindows. The repo transport is POSIX-only (git rev-parse subshell, [ -n $r ], exec). Scoped out in plan.md:153 / cell 6a:70 — but only in HISTORY docs. The runtime contract of record, docs/specs/hook-runtime.md Open Gaps (:137-147), does NOT name Windows or non-POSIX $SHELL, and the spec's own rule (:44) says a coverage gap must be logged+listed. A Windows/fish/nu Codex user adopting this public trusted checked-in contract gets transport-layer failures with no declared gap. FIX (doc only): add one Open Gaps line — 'Repo-target Codex transport is POSIX-$SHELL only; Windows/commandWindows and non-POSIX shells are an unimplemented coverage gap.'

title: Review P3 batch: catalog/harness polish (magic-9, genericRow drops skip, loose apply_patch judge, required-list gaps, TDZ, no --write, live-state race, unquoted echo)
detail: From code-quality + test-coverage + architecture reviewers. (1) test_hook_contracts.mjs:690 and :1537 hardcode length===9 — derive from renderProjection('codex',{target:repo}). (2) genericRow (:129) drops the skip field so route-plugin-census prints 'ok' not 'SKIP' when codex CLI absent — mirror codexAcceptanceRow:738 to propagate skip:Boolean(extra.skip). (3) expectApplyPatchDenied (:299-311) passes on exit2+any-stderr — also assert stderr names the .bee/state.json target. (4) codex-repo-target-transport not in REQUIRED_CATALOG_ROW_IDS (:92) — add it. (5) routeExpectation (:1276) reads ADVISORY_ROUTE_EVENTS declared 6 lines below (:1282) — move const up. (6) no --write/render script to regenerate .codex/hooks.json — contributor must hand-run node -e; add scripts/render-hooks.mjs. (7) route-live-state-untouched (:1760) hashes live .bee/state.json across ~40 spawns — flakes if a real bee hook fires meanwhile; scope to mtime/inode per spawn. (8) root/nested state-sync rows prove only exit 0 — extend the fixture-state-mutation check from route-stop-handler:state-sync to the other arms.

title: Gate 4 for Codex repo-fallback slice (6a+6b) DEFERRED by human — merge NOT approved, resume later
detail: Human said 'process tiếp việc khác, việc này để sau' at Gate 4. Review verdict stands: 2 reproduced P1s block merge (P1-a fail-open kill-switch via git config core.bare true; P1-b nested-git-root silent bypass), both closed by one fix cell codex-parity-6c (PreToolUse fails CLOSED exit 2 + existence check [ -f $r/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs ]; advisory hooks keep exit 0; amend spec R2/B6; fold in P3 injection-hardening). Plus a Gate-4 UAT item: run a real Codex apply_patch against .bee/state.json and observe deny — settles whether matcher Edit|Write|..|Grep excluding apply_patch voids the slice's safety claim (=> P1 if it does not fire). review gate NOT set; execution gate for 6c NOT created. Cells 6a (f0860ac) + 6b (7499a71) are committed and green but UNMERGED pending 6c + UAT. RESUME: create cell 6c, return to validating. Full report: docs/history/codex-runtime-parity/reports/review-codex-repo-fallback.md

title: bee_cells.mjs has no revise/update verb — reworking a failed-validation cell in place needs a hand-edit
detail: codex-parity-6c failed validation and had to be re-shaped after a Gate-2 re-plan. addCell throws on an existing id (any status, incl. dropped), and dropCell keeps the file, so the id cannot be re-added. Per hive law 11 the in-place edit is filed here first. A 'revise --id --file' verb (or add --force to overwrite a dropped cell) would remove the hand-edit.

title: extractBashTargets does not model dd/install/python-open/rsync — governed Bash writes bypass the write guard
detail: Red-team of codex-parity-6c round-3 confirmed: guards.mjs extractBashTargets extracts no target for dd of=, install, python -c open(...,'w'), rsync — so those governed Bash writes reach neither the gate/reservation guard nor the new marker-forge deny. Pre-existing (not introduced by 6c) but it means the above-root marker plant is achievable with no symlink. 6c documents it as a bounded D2 Open Gap; closing it (modeling these primitives) is a separate cell with its own blast radius.

title: bee_cells has no update/replace verb: drop keeps the file so re-add of the same id is impossible via CLI
detail: Cell-review repairs (the normal validating loop) need drop+re-add of the same cell id; addCell refuses because the dropped cell file persists. Need an update verb or add --replace. Hand-deleting the dropped file used as the sanctioned fallback per hive rule 11.

title: No CLI verb edits .bee/config.json keys
detail: Removing the retired advisor key from .bee/config.json has no CLI path (bee_state owns state.json only); hand-edit used per hive rule 11 after this log line.

title: Partial-sync window: onboard --apply can transiently pair a NEW helper with an OLD lib (missing named export hard-error), self-heals on re-apply
detail: api-contract review of fanout-delegation: inherent to any cross-file export addition in the vendored twin set; helpers are planned before lib modules. Consider apply-order (libs before consumers) or a one-shot retry in onboard.

title: Rescue ladder rung 1.5: answer-in-place — resume the same blocked worker (SendMessage) for missing-information blockers instead of fresh re-dispatch
detail: Owner-agreed 2026-07-12. [BLOCKED] re-dispatch discards worker progress. For missing-info blockers (file path, decision quote, constraint) orchestrator resumes the SAME worker with the answer, keeping its context; wrong-approach blockers still re-dispatch fresh (stale assumptions). Advisor stays the orchestrator/session model per decision 0015 — no new role, no config.

title: Contain bee footprint: .spikes/ lives at repo root outside .bee/ — user expects everything bee creates in one box
detail: Same user report 2026-07-12. Options: (a) move spikes to .bee/spikes/ — touches bee-validating, bee-exploring, bee-planning, bee-hive routing table, AGENTS.block.md and the write-guard allowlist; (b) cheaper: onboard-managed gitignore for .spikes/ so it never surfaces. Decide together with the P2 gitignore friction — one onboarding change can cover both.

title: Host-repo users see constant .bee json churn: onboarding never manages .gitignore, so mutable runtime files land in git status/IDE while the human works
detail: Reported by a real host-repo user 2026-07-12: bee's file noise (state.json, logs/, reservations.json, workers/, capture-queue.jsonl) breaks their focus — unexplained json changes appear continuously. Onboard --apply should write ignore rules for machine-local mutable files; decide explicitly which files are team-durable (decisions.jsonl, backlog.jsonl, cells?) vs local-only. No gitignore handling exists anywhere in skills/bee-hive today (grep verified).

title: bee repo .gitignore is corrupt: two entries merged into one line (.bee/feedback-digest.json.spikes/) so neither works; both targets already committed anyway
detail: xxd shows missing newline between .bee/feedback-digest.json and .spikes/. Because the ignore never worked, .bee/feedback-digest.json and 187 .spikes/ files are git-tracked in this repo. Real fix = split the line AND git rm --cached the tracked targets — owner call, same decision as the P2 onboarding-gitignore friction.

title: extractBashTargets false-positives during validating: git -C <spike-dir> add/rm and unresolved $VAR args are flagged as repo-root write targets, blocking legitimate .spikes/ probes
detail: bee-footprint validation 2026-07-12: three consecutive blocks on a spike-local probe (var $P, then .gitignore redirect after cd, then git add tracked.txt via -C). Workaround: wrap the probe in a node script under .spikes/ and execute it. Parser could resolve cwd/-C context or skip args of git add/rm --cached.

title: No standing byte-identity sweep covers the hooks twin pair (hooks/ vs .bee/bin/hooks/) — only templates/*.mjs and templates/lib/*.mjs are swept
detail: Surfaced by bee-footprint plan-checker W1 (2026-07-12): a one-sided hook edit passes every suite. footprint-2 mitigates locally by grepping .bee/bin in its verify; the durable fix is extending the test_lib sweep to hooks pairs.

title: bee-validating SKILL.md prescribes 'bee_state.mjs set --phase validated' but the phase enum has no 'validated' (only validating/swarming...)
detail: Hit at bee-footprint Gate 3 (2026-07-12): the documented handoff command fails; fallback used --phase swarming. Either add 'validated' to the enum or fix the three skill docs that reference it.

title: Parallel swarm workers share one git index: bob's footprint-2 commit swept stuart's already-staged footprint-1 trace files into the wrong commit
detail: bee-footprint wave 1 (2026-07-12): content intact, caps correct, but commit attribution crossed because both workers stage in the same working tree. Options: workers commit via explicit pathspec only (stuart did; bob's 'git commit' without pathspec swept the index), orchestrator-owned commits at wave close, or worktree isolation for workers.

title: Write-guard DIRECT_EDIT_DENY false-positives on 'git rm --cached <governed-path>' — index-only ops are not content edits
detail: footprint-3 (2026-07-12): guard blocked the sanctioned git rm --cached .bee/state.json; worker used git update-index --force-remove (equivalent plumbing, uncaught). Guard's git add|mv|rm pattern should special-case --cached/index-only forms or honor cell-scope sanctions.

title: Tradeoff note: .bee/spikes/ is write-allowlisted (.bee/ prefix) AND gitignored — staged content there escapes git-status/review visibility; do not read clean git status as nothing-staged
footprint-2's stored verify grep false-positives on re-run (origin-comment strings + the D2 denial test fixture) — capped green, re-runs red; tighten the predicate if the cell is ever re-verified
AGENTS.md marker functions still use unanchored substring matching — apply the same line-anchored hardening footprint-4 gives the gitignore splice, for parity
bee_cells.mjs has no update verb — validation repair loops must hand-edit .bee/cells/*.json after filing friction (rule 11 fallback used by review-on-demand slice 1)

detail: Structured audits read trace.deviations, not prose; cap step should append matching entries.

title: Extend unified dispatcher and registry to bee_state, bee_backlog, bee_capture, bee_reviews, bee_feedback
detail: Follow-up to DA6 (decision 30606de4): Phase 1 covered 4 legacy helpers + cells.update. Phase 2 extends coverage to bee_state, bee_backlog, bee_capture, bee_reviews, bee_feedback via identical dispatcher + registry + bijection-test pattern.

title: CLI parity check can pass when both producers fail
detail: Capture each CLI separately; assert exit status and output shape before diffing stdout. Current process-substitution diff suppresses producer failures.

title: Live-validate bee openai.yaml metadata UX
detail: Official docs define the fields but no length limit was established; run representative bee metadata through the real Codex/ChatGPT loader and confirm long short_description handling before treating UI compatibility as fully proved.

title: Harden metadata projection lifecycle census
detail: Add top-level bee-* file/symlink/broken-link cases, a surviving orphan projection case, and render-twice full-tree byte equality; current fixtures cover directories and stale recovery but not these entry classes explicitly.

title: High-risk lane: independent clean-context verifier subagent after goal-check
detail: Ref docs/REFs Fable-5 article step 6: maker self-critique biased; verifier sees only diff+must_haves, no worker reasoning. Scope to high-risk lane only to stay lean.

title: Vision-verify for UI cells: screenshot in verification_evidence checked by vision
detail: Ref article step 13: text-only verify misses visual regressions. UI-touching cells may attach screenshot; orchestrator/verifier checks it against goal with vision.

detail: Ref article step 10 (5-stage memory): extend bee-xia evidence labeling to learnings frontmatter so consult-stage readers know what was actually checked.

detail: Ref article step 14: safety-classifier declines look like real errors; correct handling is route to another tier/model or surface to human, never rerun same tier.

title: Remove LEGACY_HELPER_RE transition guard from hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs
detail: shim-retire (decision bbc6bcea D3) kept LEGACY_HELPER_RE (bee-write-guard.mjs ~line 130-145) so hosts mid-upgrade with old vendored .bee/bin/*.mjs shims still resolve legacy CLI-shape command strings. Remove the regex, its branch in resolveCliCommandName/checkCliShape, and the associated comment once hosts have had a full release cycle to re-onboard past shim-retire (bee.mjs is now the sole shipped CLI).

detail: shim-retire-1 deleted 7 parity-vs-shim checks; explicit replacements were recorded for 2 (frozen-key-refusal, cells-ready). Audit that status --json/text, reservations list --active-only, decisions active still have equivalent behavioral assertions via runBee; add any that lost coverage.

detail: shim-retire-1 deleted 7 parity-vs-shim checks; explicit replacements were recorded for 2. Audit that status --json/text, reservations list --active-only, decisions active still have equivalent behavioral assertions via runBee; add any that lost coverage.

title: write-guard false-positive: git add/commit of CLI-owned .bee jsonl is blocked as a direct edit
detail: Staging a CLI-appended .bee/backlog.jsonl (or decisions/review-candidates) into a git commit trips the direct-edit guard, so close-out commits cannot include the durable jsonl records; sessions leave them dirty (observed this session and the previous one). git add mutates no file content — the guard should exempt read-only git staging of CLI-owned paths or provide a bee-blessed commit verb.

title: No CLI verb for .bee/config.json commands.* (verify/test) — must hand-edit
test_onboard_bee.mjs writes to ./undefined/cfgrepo in the live tree (undefined path var) — leaves untracked garbage
bee state handoff has no clear verb

title: Parallelism ceiling by design: file-partitioning is convention, conflicts deny-and-stall, no plan-time overlap/graph pass

Confirmed by code walk: (1) cells.files is free text, never validated for cross-cell overlap (lib/cells.mjs validateNewCell); (2) no dep-graph/cycle/critical-path analysis at plan or validate time — overlap discovered only at write time via reservation/hold DENY (guards.mjs checkWrite); a denied worker sits [BLOCKED] until orchestrator rescue — framework has no queue/wait primitive, only deny; (3) wave grouping is manual orchestrator judgment per wave (bee-swarming SKILL.md:32) so conservative partitioning serializes; (4) workers share one checkout/git index — one commit per cell, no worktree isolation, commit contention undiscussed. Candidate fixes: plan/validate-time file-overlap matrix + dep-graph pass computing waves automatically (cells already declare files); optional per-worker worktree isolation; queue instead of pure deny. Compare: task-intake harness with acyclic store + write-time validation (Van's port experiment); bead_rust_viewer plan-splitting algorithm.

Root cause: the internal decision-citation vocabulary (bee's anti-drift spine — locked decisions cited never reinterpreted) is bleeding into the human layer instead of the plain-language translation the Gate Presentation Contract + rule 11 require. Fix is NOT to remove the IDs (load-bearing internally) but to enforce the translation layer at the point of speaking to the user. Proposed enforcement: a nudge/hook flagging user-facing output that matches /\b(D|AO)\d+\b|decision [0-9a-f]{8}/ when the turn is not a user-asked-about-bee turn. Secondary signals same report: (a) self-prompt-editing during onboarding felt scary -> surface the managed-marker/versioned/consent-gated scope more transparently; (b) POSITIVE: 2-worktree parallel 'ngồi thiết kế trong lúc đứa kia làm' working as intended (P44 worktree-feature-parallelism).

title: ID-leak is NOT a prose gap: 3 pressure tests (incl. unprimed realistic immersion) on generation tier all GREEN — re-diagnose toward version skew / about-bee turn / non-chat surface

Iron Law RED phase could not produce a failing test: three scenarios (progress update, gate presentation, justify-a-choice; plus a hardened run that immersed the agent in D-ID/AO/hash reasoning with rule 11 buried in a manual; plus a fully UNPRIMED run with no leak/audit mention) all translated every locked-decision id into plain user language with zero leakage. Closing argument: only the orchestrator speaks to the user, and the orchestrator is always the strongest (ceiling) model — workers return digests, never user chat — so a generation-tier test is representative-or-conservative for the real surface. Therefore the current Silent Bookkeeping prose + litmus is sufficient WHEN PRESENT. Van's real-world leak is most likely (a) VERSION SKEW: host predates the litmus-strengthened rule 11 -> fix is onboarding Van's host to current bee, not editing prose; or (b) it was an about-bee turn (codes legitimately allowed); or (c) a non-chat surface (status line / auto-report shown inline).
