cells claim is non-atomic RMW — swarm workers can double-claim; worker flow must ride the O_EXCL claim path
detail: Audit 2026-07-19 confirmed: claimCell (templates/lib/cells.mjs:388-424) is read-check-write with no O_EXCL claim file; only claim-next -> claimCellFile (claims.mjs:237-265, flag wx) is race-safe. bee-executing SKILL.md:39 + worker-details.md:14 instruct workers to run the unsafe 'cells claim', and bee-swarming has no lease around schedule->assign->spawn, so two independent orchestrator sessions can dispatch the same cell and both workers believe they claimed it. Fix shape (P0 from audit): orchestrator claims via claim-next/claim-file WITH session id BEFORE spawning; worker receives cell_id+claim owner and validates instead of calling cells claim; retire or claim-file-back 'cells claim'.

title: Reservations: shared-JSON RMW drops concurrent holds, and worker flow omits --session so cross-session guard never sees them
detail: Audit 2026-07-19 confirmed: reservations.mjs reserve() (110-140) is read-whole-store -> conflict-check -> append -> write-whole-store; two concurrent reserves both pass the check against the same snapshot and last writer silently drops the other's reservation (writeJsonAtomic prevents corruption only). Separately --session is optional (bee.mjs:823-834), session-less rows are excluded from findSessionConflicts (reservations.mjs:97-108), and bee-executing SKILL.md:44/worker-details.md:15 never pass --session — so worker reservations are invisible to the cross-session write guard. Fix shape (P0): per-hold O_EXCL files (.bee/holds/<hash>.json) or equivalent transactional store; session id auto-derived from runtime context, not an optional flag.

title: Cell mutators (verify/cap/block/unclaim/reopen) have no claim-owner check — any session can cap another session's cell
detail: Audit 2026-07-19 confirmed: recordVerify/capCell/blockCell/unclaimCell/reopenCell (templates/lib/cells.mjs:426-620) do readCell -> checks -> writeCell with no session-id/claim-token proof against the claim file's session. Fix shape: these verbs require the caller's session to match the active claim (or an explicit --force with audit decision).

title: Session heartbeat refreshed only at SessionStart — live session reads stale after 15min, opening its lane to claim-next poaching
detail: Audit 2026-07-19 confirmed (and cnt-1/cnt-2 race root cause, decision 0101ec31): heartbeatSession is called only from hooks/bee-session-init.mjs (SessionStart matcher); UserPromptSubmit and PostToolUse state-sync never refresh it. DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_STALE_SECONDS=900 vs claim TTL 3600 (claims.mjs:33-34). A session heads-down >15min looks dead to the GH#20 live-owner lane guard, so other sessions pool its lane's open cells. Fix shape (P1 from audit): refresh heartbeat in UserPromptSubmit/PostToolUse (throttled ~60s) and renew active claim + hold TTLs alongside. Complements existing 2026-07-19 'claim handshake broke under heartbeat lag' row.

title: state.json logical RMW is last-writer-wins — needs revision/CAS or lock (already named cnr2-5 future concern)
detail: Audit 2026-07-19 re-confirmed what test_state_write_concurrency.mjs:24-33 documents as HONEST LIMIT: writeState is whole-object atomic-rename only; two sessions' logical merges clobber each other (workers list, lanes, last_activity). Fix shape: revision field + compare-and-swap retry, or a lock; scoped in CONTEXT D-cnr2-5. Elevate when multi-session-hardening lands.

title: Orchestrator-handed session id in worker dispatch mismatches worker's real CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID — self-hold false-positive
detail: cnt-6 worker (2026-07-19): dispatch prompt carried the orchestrator's presumed session id 6d0892b6, worker's actual runtime CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID was 85b83b0f; write-guard compared the hold's session field against the real payload session id and denied the worker's own git add as a cross-session hold. Workaround: workers read their own CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID via env and reserve with that. Real fix rides the multi-session-hardening item: session id auto-derived from runtime context at reserve time, never passed by hand.

title: Re-run V3 probe when a codex build accepts elevation again; Run A hand-rolled vs onboarded hook-discovery divergence unresolved
detail: probe-evidence.md Open follow-up: V3 (PreToolUse envelope with override fields) is terminal-UNOBSERVED on 0.144.4 (hand-rolled fixture never fired despite 3 accepted spawns — root cause open) and 0.144.6 (V1 refused server-side, turn never reaches tool execution). When a future build accepts hide_spawn_agent_metadata elevation, run canary_codex.mjs --probe with the onboarded-chain fixture; a positive V3 unlocks the deferred D6 route-check (decision 350f1e82).

title: Freeze plan.md at Gate 2; move current slice to a separate slice record referencing the approved plan
detail: bee-planning SKILL.md:89 mutates the approved plan.md in place (requirements-only -> implementation-ready) after Gate 2; approved artifact should be immutable, slice = execution state (.bee/ or cells slice_id), not strategy doc content

title: Tiny/small should not require plan.md: tiny = request + cell; small = short scoping synthesis + cells
detail: Current tiny fast path still requires plan.md direct note, dispatched worker, done-report (bee-hive SKILL.md:118, briefing:34); cell already carries files/action/verify — it IS the micro-plan

title: Ask merged Gate 2+3 AFTER the exact work packet (cells) exists, not before Prep
detail: bee-planning SKILL.md:83-87: approval precedes '6. Prep (after Gate 2 approval only)' which creates cells + exact files/verify — execution approval granted before the execution package exists; reorder to preview-cells -> reality-check -> merged approval -> persist

title: Mode-gate file caps lack a 'product files only' carve-out; bee artifacts can inflate counts past tiny/small
detail: No exclusion language anywhere in skills/ (grep confirmed); define count = production/test/config files of the behavior change, excluding .bee/**, docs/history/**, plan/brief/spec sync

title: Risk flags 'existing covered behavior' + 'weak proof around the area' promote ordinary covered bugfixes to standard
detail: bee-hive SKILL.md:98/106: 2 flags -> standard; a normal bugfix on tested behavior before running tests trivially hits both; narrow to 'changes a previously tested public behavior' / 'requires weakening existing proof'

title: Planning bootstrap (full context reads) runs before the mode gate; cheap intake classification should come first
detail: bee-planning SKILL.md sections 1->3: CONTEXT/patterns/decisions/learnings/status all read before lane is known; tiny should pay only targeted reads — classify first, scale bootstrap to lane

title: Stale .bee/lanes records leak gate flags into reads: gates flipped true uncommanded after feature switch
detail: Observed 2026-07-19 ~11:19-11:21: after state set to new feature lane-ceremony-v3, successive reads showed shape then execution flip true without any gate command; .bee/lanes/{perf-jsonl,perf-log,release-1-3-3}.json hold exactly context:false,shape:true,execution:true. Suspect resolvePipeline record selection mixing stale lane with state.json (split-brain read). Multi-session-hardening domain.

title: Pre-verify dirty-tree guard: blocking verify aborts with a named conflict when out-of-scope paths are dirty in a shared checkout
detail: Mechanize learnings 20260719-lane-ceremony-v3 rule 1: before a cell's blocking verify chain runs while another live session occupies the checkout, git status --short scoped outside the cell files[] must be clean, else abort/defer naming the conflicting paths+session instead of burning the full suite into a deterministic red (evidence: lcv3-5 blocked twice by msh mid-edit lib).

title: Direct-edit guard blocks git add of CLI-owned .bee jsonl, so bookkeeping cannot be committed
detail: git add .bee/backlog.jsonl (staging, not editing) is denied by the direct-edit guard; append-only jsonl rows end up permanently uncommitted from sessions that honor the guard. Related to P46 (git exemption shape).

title: Mechanize the derived-artifact set for lib-touching cells (3rd recurrence: manifest 20260715, projections cnt-7, plugin trees msh-1)
detail: Any cell whose files touch skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/ (or hooks/) must carry mirror sync + render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs + release_manifest --write/--check in its verify. Three prose recurrences prove the reminder does not hold. Candidate mechanisms: cells add/update validation that derives required verify fragments from the files list; or a judge rule flagging lib-touch cells whose verify lacks the regen commands; or a pre-cap hook check. Owner: next grooming/evolving pass.

title: Worker prompt template: worktree dispatches must call EnterWorktree first (plain cd leaves write-guard anchored to main)
detail: Observed live (worker carl, msh-1): Bash cd into the worktree did not re-anchor hook root resolution; writes denied until the worker called the EnterWorktree tool. Later msh prompts carried the instruction manually. Fold into bee-swarming references/swarming-reference.md worker prompt template (one line) in a future docs cell.

title: Unreproduced write-guard denials of read-only grep commands — capture tool_input bytes on next occurrence
detail: During compounding analysis two plain grep commands (glob + 2>/dev/null) were denied with 'could not be canonically contained'; replaying the same strings through extractBashTargets returned empty paths (no repro). Next occurrence: read .bee/logs/hooks.jsonl for the exact tool_input.command bytes and diff against the typed command before filing a fix.

title: bump_version.mjs does not re-render plugin skill trees — 4th derived-artifact recurrence, in the release flow itself
detail: Bump 1.7.0 changed BEE_VERSION in templates/lib/state.mjs; rendered plugin trees kept the old bytes; plugin_distribution failed 2 checks at release verify. Fix applied manually (render + manifest --write). Mechanize: bump_version.mjs should run render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs + release_manifest --write itself — fold into the existing mechanization backlog item for lib-touch derived artifacts.

title: Semantic judge auto-scaled by risk policy — today semantic review is user-invoked only
detail: BJM assessment 2026-07-20: default chain ends verified-but-unreviewed for every lane (decision 565e68d0 made review user-invoked deliberately). Proposal: risk-policy judge matrix — tiny/small mechanical only; standard + semantic checklist judge (must_haves/CONTEXT/task-to-diff alignment, structured verdict schema with failure_signature + fixability, never free prose); high-risk + independent-model judge automatically. Judge verdict schema and model/context-independence record (builder_model vs judge_model, effective route confirmed vs unverified — dispatch economics already logs pinned/unverified, reuse it) belong to this item.

title: Global cell-lifetime stop conditions — budgets currently reset per claim
detail: BJM assessment: consult budget is per-claim by design (rescue rung 1 grants a fresh budget), so claim->block->re-dispatch can loop indefinitely at cell lifetime. Proposal: hard caps recorded on the CELL (max_claims_per_cell, max_attempts, max_total_consults, max_same_failure_signature=2, wall-clock ceiling); exceeding any => forced stop with full attempt history surfaced. No token ceilings without usage telemetry — count attempts/consults/wall-clock instead.

title: Revision ledger per cell: attempt history with failure signatures so retries never repeat a failed approach blind
detail: BJM assessment: trace records outcome/evidence/deviations but not attempt-by-attempt {input_commit, output_commit, judge_verdict, failure_signature, approach_taken, progress}. Manager cannot see whether a re-dispatch is progressing or looping. Same-signature repeat after 2 attempts with insignificant diff delta => early stop (feeds the stop-conditions item).

title: Judge-standard sufficiency: verify command minimum derived from change type, not worker honesty
detail: BJM assessment: verify must be runnable (enforced) but nothing checks it is SUFFICIENT — 'npm run lint' passes while behavior is wrong. Proposal: JUDGE_STANDARD_INSUFFICIENT check computed from cell type/risk: formatting=>lint/type; bugfix=>regression test reproducing the old failure; behavior_change=>red-before/green-after (red_failure_evidence exists — mechanize the check instead of trusting the declaration); API=>contract test; auth/security=>negative-path tests; migration=>forward+rollback. Natural home: cells add/update validation + judge.

title: All-clean friction records across a high-risk lane cannot distinguish asked-and-clean from never-asked
detail: Compounding analyst: scl-1..5 all show deviations:[] friction:null single-pass attempts — statistically either genuinely clean or friction-recording not exercised. Candidate: worker report template gains an explicit friction: none-found line (affirmative), or spot-check discipline noted in swarming goal-check.

title: Goal-check judge fires by doctrine only — mechanize the dispatch once exercised by a few features
detail: cells judge-record has full mechanical wiring + one live production record (scl-2, PASS, independence confirmed) but the dispatch itself is orchestrator prose in the goal-check step. After 2-3 features exercise it, consider a chain-nudge hook reminder or a judge-pending signal in status for capped behavior_change cells without a semantic_judge entry in standard/high-risk lanes.

title: 1.7.3 roadmap (GH #27): judge lifecycle verbs, session-id neutrality, per-cell CAS, stale sweep, worktree mutex, small serial loop, tiny-direct, ps1 rollback, Windows E2E
dispatch prepare --kind cell emits read-only bee-gather agent type for execution cells (GH #27 item 5 confirmed live 2026-07-20)
template changes need render_plugin_skill_trees + manifest --write mid-feature or verify goes red (bit gh-issue-fixes-172); fold render into onboard --apply?
write-guard CLI-shape check intercepts mutating-verb --help before the new group-help path answers (found dogfooding GH #23 fix)
bump_version omits plugin-tree render (6th recurrence)
Crash recovery ignores harness transcripts: post-crash resume rebuilds only from decisions/state/cells, losing unsettled in-conversation context
detail: User field report (2026-07-20): bee's decisions/specs/patterns are lossy interpolation, not full truth — in-session coherence comes from the live conversation context, and a mid-run machine shutdown (no HANDOFF written) loses everything not yet settled into a decision. Claude Code already persists full transcripts at ~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl; bee lacks a crash-recovery affordance that mines the broken session's transcript as a SECONDARY reconciliation source against decisions/state (decisions stay the primary memory — never load raw transcripts as context). Complements capture stubs (decision 0017), which shrink the unsettled window from the other side.

title: cap on transcript-recovery-1 printed JUDGE_STANDARD_INSUFFICIENT: red_failure_evidence floor skipped via deliberate_exceptions door (F5) for a genuinely brand-new module
Cap-time guard: block a release-version-tuple bump not authorized by the cell's action
detail: transcript-recovery cell 4 silently ran bump_version 1.7.6; the 2 plugin.json tuple members committed (matched .claude-plugin/* glob), the 2 state.mjs members drifted uncommitted (matched no glob), invisible in commit diff + [DONE] report, caught only at feature-close by orchestrator git-status re-inspect. Mechanize (failure-analyst finding): at cell cap, run release-tuple.checkTupleEquality(); if the live tuple version != last released AND the cell action text does not match /bump.?version|release \d/i, block cap with 'unauthorized version bump in scope'. Cheaper than a general frozen-judge content diff; catches at cap not close. Also consider: release_manifest --write should refuse/warn unless the renderer ran and shows zero drift (render->manifest ordering enforcement).

title: cap-then-commit-then-release doctrine
detail: swarm entanglement observed 2026-07-20: hardening-3 capped + released its reservation but had NOT committed; xwh-1 had to hand-isolate hunks via git apply --cached and exclude aggregate regen artifacts (render sidecars, release-manifest, onboarding.json). Doctrine candidate: cap->commit->THEN release reservation; aggregate artifacts belong to a consolidated sync pass, not per-cell commits.

title: write-guard canonical-containment false positives on long multi-line commands
detail: Multiple workers + orchestrator hit 'targets could not be canonically contained' denials for plain in-repo relative paths when commands were long/multi-line (heredocs, multi-path git add, compound cat). Workaround: split into small single-purpose commands. Extractor likely over-splits tokens on newlines/quotes. Observed 2026-07-20 across exec-xwh2 and orchestrator session.

title: bee-write-guard hook dispatch returns allow where deny expected (test_bee_write_guard_hook 9/21 fail)
detail: Surfaced by cs-4 suite auto-discovery 2026-07-20: skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs was never registered in the old hand-written SUITES so it never ran; standalone it fails 9/21 — hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs guard-dispatch logic returns allow where the test expects deny. Excluded from discovery with dated comment. Needs its own fix-first cell: net-first (frozen green over current hook behavior), then determine test-wrong vs hook-wrong per branch. Do NOT silently trust either side.

title: extractBashTargets misfire repro: multi-segment commands with cells/lanes + bare --- token
detail: wux-2 worker reproduced against unmodified HEAD: guards.extractBashTargets misfires on unrelated multi-segment shell commands containing literal substrings cells/lanes plus a bare --- token. Concrete repro for the P2 canonical-containment false-positive row logged 2026-07-20. Grooming cell candidate.

title: reservations release lacks --path scoping
detail: Observed 2026-07-21 during deadlock resolution: exec-hardening-7 needed to release ONE path (templates/bee.mjs) but reservations release only scopes by --agent/--cell, so all 8 holds dropped and 7 had to be re-reserved (race window). Add --path to release exactly one reservation row.

title: verify run inside a nested clone rewrote the PARENT repo's git origin
detail: 2026-07-21 release flow: after running run_verify.mjs inside .bee/spikes/<clone>, the MAIN repo's remote.origin.url had been rewritten to the spike path (push went to the phantom remote; also earlier the spike clone dirs vanished after runs). Some suite performs git remote set-url / destructive git ops with an upward-resolved root or wrong cwd when the repo lives nested inside another repo's tree. Find the suite (candidates: installers E2E, worktree CLI/store tests), pin its cwd/root, and add a regression test that a nested-clone verify leaves the parent's remotes byte-identical.

title: CI red on tag v1.7.9: test_state needs hermetic session env; windows.yml still runs deleted test_lib.mjs
detail: Linux runs 29787111002/29787180079 red on Node 18/20/22: test_state.mjs:546-568 creates live sessions then reserves sessionless; reservations.mjs:143-149 returns SESSION_REQUIRED so no hold exists and assertRejects fails. Passes locally ONLY because inherited CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID resolves a session (verified: env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID => FAIL). Fix test hermeticity + decide guard semantics. Windows runs 29787110946/29787180137 red on Node 20/22: windows.yml:57-58 invokes skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs which no longer exists (MODULE_NOT_FOUND), skipping portable-paths/config steps. No green run exists on main.

title: test_claim_race negative control flaky (~30% local fail): sleep-based ordering, no barrier
detail: scripts/test_claim_race.mjs (g-unsafe) negative control: DETECTOR DID NOT BITE, final status claimed. 3/10 local failures; external reviewer measured 17/20. Replace 50ms-sleep ordering with a deterministic barrier.

title: worktree-admin mutex void when verify >30s: fixed stale takeover, no heartbeat/liveness
detail: lock.mjs STALE_MS=30_000 (L23, not configurable), mtime-only tryStaleTakeover L117-134, pid never liveness-checked, lockfile never refreshed. worktree-store.mjs mergeFeatureWorktree holds worktree-admin (L966) across spawnSync verify (L1085) + git spawns, violating lock.mjs L8-11 contract. Mitigation noted: MERGE_HEAD/isTreeDirty (L1001-1003) usually stops a second merge from corrupting main, but the mutex guarantee itself is breakable. Fix: lease with owner token + heartbeat, or lock only short metadata transactions. No test covers a live holder >30s.

title: cross-worktree hold acquisition is check-then-act; 1h TTL never renewed
detail: bee.mjs handleReservationsReserve: findForeignHolds (L1187, unlocked pure read) -> local reserve (L1207, different store/lock) -> mirrorHold (L1227, cross-worktree-holds lock, append-only). No atomic check-and-insert under one main-root lock: two worktrees can both see empty ledger and both hold the same path (presented to users as a hard block, bee.mjs L1200-1202). worktree-holds.mjs DEFAULT_TTL_SECONDS=3600, no renewal (header L23-24) so a live >1h worker loses protection. Also stale header claims module is UNWIRED (L1-2,37-39) — false since xwh-2. test_worktree_holds_race races distinct paths only; same-path race untested.

title: archive vs mutators unserialized (cells-archive lock != cells:<id>); 4 mutators lack archived guard; no crash-safe transaction
detail: cells.mjs: archiveFeature/unarchiveFeature take cells-archive (L570/L641) while mutators take cells:<id> — distinct lockfiles, no mutual exclusion; comment L565-569 claiming mutators can never interleave is false. assertNotArchived (L404-408) is TOCTOU vs archive rename (L598). reopenCell L1417, setTier L1452, resetCellBudget L1783, recordJudgeVerdict L1866 all lack assertNotArchived while readCell falls back to archive (L505-517) and writeCell writes active (L693) => archived cell resurrected as active duplicate. Rollback catches sync exceptions only; summary write outside try (L622-625); no destination-collision preflight (L597-598, L658-659); unarchive renameSync can overwrite an active file. Fix direction: unify lock order, guard in writeCell not per-mutator, journal/staging + crash recovery.

title: Codex session identity not end-to-end: hook writes session record but CLI never reads it; transcript_path dropped
detail: claims.mjs resolveSessionId L73-80 (flag -> BEE_SESSION_ID -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID) never consults .bee/sessions/; bee-session-init.mjs L58-69 persists the Codex session_id but no wrapper exports it, so solo Codex sessions hit SESSION_REQUIRED (claims.mjs L321-326) because isConcurrentMode counts the session's OWN live record (test_claims.mjs:290 documents this). Fix: durable session-context fallback in resolveSessionId (own fresh record => self-exclusion or adoption). Recovery: transcript_path from hook payload never persisted/used; perf.mjs L45-48 assumes Claude layout for every root; test_recovery.mjs:553-567 'Codex' test is a relabeled Claude tree.

title: Windows Codex hooks break from nested cwd: commandWindows is cwd-relative, no git-root resolution
detail: .codex/hooks.json: POSIX command resolves git rev-parse --show-toplevel; commandWindows is bare 'node .bee/bin/hooks/... --source=repo' anchored on session cwd => MODULE_NOT_FOUND from docs/ or src/. Generators: hooks/catalog.mjs:97-99 and onboard_bee.mjs:2174-2176. NOTE: test_hook_contracts.mjs:1129-1149 contract-checks commandWindows to REQUIRE the bare relative form (no shell metachars), so the fix must change the contract too (e.g. node -e resolver or a repo-root .cmd shim); nested-cwd is exercised for POSIX only (:2803,:3077).

title: judge NEEDS_REVISION reopen leaves stale verify_passed=true and no owner; PASS-then-recap reuses old evidence
detail: cells.mjs recordJudgeVerdict L1885-1898 flips capped->claimed without releaseTrace (L1373-1383) and without creating a claim; trace.worker/claim_session stale. claimCell (L1072) accepts open only and reopenCell (L1423) refuses claimed => only unclaimCell path recovers (works, clears verify) but nothing routes there. Real bite: after reopen, record PASS verdict -> capCell gate (L1178 verify_passed===true; D-GHF-C L1191 satisfied) re-caps on the PREVIOUS attempt's verify with no provenance/attempt check. 2195e72 added test_misc.mjs:2619 asserting only the status flip — locks in the bug. Fix: NEEDS_REVISION => open + releaseTrace + require fresh verify; secondary: dispatch-prepare nickname prepare-<id> vs real owner (L109), --force-ownership audits but does not transfer claim (L243-253).

title: cap helper: any non-empty deliberate_exceptions skips the red_failure_evidence floor check entirely
detail: Reported by 1710-3 worker: including any deliberate_exceptions entry (even an unrelated note) bypasses the red-evidence length/duplicate floor at cap. Floor should apply unless the exception explicitly targets the evidence requirement.

title: dispatch-prepare --force-ownership stays advisory-only: no simple transfer primitive on the trace.worker axis
4 template suites fail on real Windows: worktree gitdir pointer path, write-guard hook, cells, misc
detail: First-ever real Windows run of the template suites (1710-1 windows.yml fix) exposed pre-existing portability bugs: test_bee_cli (linked worktree reverse gitdir pointer mismatch on C:\ paths; doctor version wording; recovery scan/window rows; registry example execution), test_bee_write_guard_hook, test_cells, test_misc. Excluded explicitly from the windows-portable subset for v1.7.10-rc (annotated in windows.yml). Fix = make these Windows-portable (path normalization likely the shared root cause), then re-include. Runs: 29799623217.

title: test_claim_race scenario (d) unwind assertion flakes under CI load (Node 22 job)
detail: Run 29800232965: (d) 'no claim-store file may survive the race — every refused racer must unwind its own O_EXCL acquisition (Δ2)' failed on Node 22 while 18/20 green on the same commit. Likely the assert races the last refused racer's unwind. Make deterministic: wait for every racer process to fully exit (collect all exit codes) before asserting the claim-store state; same barrier discipline as (g)/(c).

title: Rare genuine mutual-exclusion violation in lock.mjs tryStaleTakeover under heavy contention
detail: During rel1710rc-3 stress runs (~1-in-10 unconstrained under concurrent box load) test_store_lock scenario (c) recorded a REAL mutual-exclusion violation; reproduced on unmodified baseline — pre-existing, not introduced by the bounded-retry fix. Needs its own investigation cell; do NOT weaken the violations assertion.

title: test_onboard_bee 'shared Worker timeout preserves captured stdout/stderr' flakes on slow CI runners
detail: Run 29803404804 verify(22): the worker-timeout row failed on Node 22 while 18/20 green on the same commit (a146dca) — third distinct CPU-timing flake class on 2-core runners this release. Make the timeout row robust: assert on collected output after the child fully exits, or widen the capture window; same discipline as the race-suite hardening (rel1710rc-3).

title: Agent-name env prefix leaks into test child processes and pollutes test_guards fixtures
detail: Worker w-rc4 ran run_verify with the agent-name env prefix per the write-heavy convention; the var leaked into test_guards.mjs child fixtures causing 3 spurious failures. run_verify is read-only proof and needs no prefix — document that, or scrub the var in test fixtures.

title: test_store_lock flaky under concurrent verify load
test_render_race negative control (a) flakes when the unguarded racers get lucky
detail: Scenario (a) is a deliberate-red control: unguarded concurrent renders must crash or tear, else (b)'s green proves nothing. On WSL2 under load, ~1-in-3 runs both racers exit 0 with intact trees -> control does not bite -> suite fails. Same class as rel1710rc-3's discipline: force the interleaving structurally (barrier/injected pause inside the unguarded proxy) instead of relying on scheduler luck. Pre-existing; unrelated to decision-propagation (render race code untouched).

title: No parity guard between .bee/bin/** and the onboarding.json managed-hash ledger
detail: A stale ledger shipped on main (commit 1aa74de) and every clean checkout reported drift:true at 1.9.0 while the files were byte-identical to source. test_lib_mirror guards templates <-> .bee/bin; nothing guards .bee/bin <-> onboarding.json, a THIRD copy relationship. Fix shape: a verify-chain check that recomputes the managed hashes and fails when the tracked ledger is stale (same idiom as release_manifest --check), so the release flow cannot tag a commit whose ledger was not refreshed. Reported by the user; erodes trust in the version signal.

title: bee status --json returns ~4000 tokens; the lanes block alone is 58% of it
detail: Measured 16,017 bytes on this repo: lanes 9,249 (58%), recent_decisions 1,683. AGENTS.md mandates status --json at every session start and after every compaction, so this is a per-session context tax larger than its latency cost (277-290ms local, ~1.1s on a loaded box per the user). Fix shape: lanes should summarise by default (count + the active one + terminal counts) with the full records behind a flag, same discipline as decisions --recent. User-reported.

title: BEE_AGENT_NAME leaks into run_verify test children
okf_migrate ANCHOR_REGISTRY has no tie to git history
verification_evidence.deliberate_exceptions silently downgrades the cap judge standard
detail: okf-7 attached a substantive red_failure_evidence AND a deliberate_exceptions note explaining a ranking-law design choice. capCell took the exceptions door: it capped with JUDGE_STANDARD_INSUFFICIENT (F5, floor not enforced) even though the evidence was well over the 80-char floor and unique. The door should only open when red_failure_evidence is absent or thin — a present, floor-passing evidence string should keep the standard enforced regardless of an exceptions note. Re-capping to fix it is impossible (capCell refuses an already-capped cell).

title: write-guard CLI-shape parser fires on command TEXT inside heredocs and node -e bodies
promote verifySummary falls back to raw evidence JSON when trace has no verify_tail key
no bee knowledge normalize verb — the round-trip warning names the fix but nothing performs it
derivePin does not require expected_counts.unparsed_blocks — the regression-catch rests on authoring convention
fidelity metric is lexical — a faithful rewrite that renames terminology scores near zero
duplicate rule ids in source: hook-runtime R14, workflow-state R19-R21
7 doctrine-layer R17 citations resolve only through the stub — they break when F3 deletes stubs
parseStubAnchorMap still restates the anchor regex despite the shared-constant claim
implement-plan retirement would lose Technical Design and Rollback Plan
context dumps all 47 critical patterns regardless of relevance — 65% of the budget is noise
one unreproducible verify red at session resume, evidence not captured
worker trace prose restated live numbers staler AND more flattering than the payload
nothing enforces that a cell's context came from the knowledge manifest
cells judge-record cannot record a NEEDS_REVISION verdict in a repo with a decision taxonomy
No census guard that new doc-tree/root resolution reuses resolveProductRoot instead of re-inventing path.join
detail: SECOND occurrence of this class: G1's bundle predicate and then G13's scribingTarget/bundleMode both joined docs paths straight off root while inject.mjs, backlog.mjs and bee-session-close.mjs already routed through resolveProductRoot. In the supported repo-divorce topology (.bee/config.json product_root) a fully migrated host is then graded bundle-less with its fallback pointing at an empty workshop docs/specs — silent knowledge loss, invisible because every fixture was single-root. Mechanizable: a census/grep suite asserting no new code path joins 'docs' off a bare root, plus a mandatory divorced-root fixture for any code that resolves a doc tree.

title: Derived-artifact set for a templates/lib touch is now FOUR deep and still unmechanized (adds the onboarding managed-hash ledger)
detail: Fourth recurrence of pattern 20260715. Editing skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/inject.mjs left four mandatory suites red at close-out. The known three (mirror copy, plugin tree render, release manifest write) are now four: .bee/onboarding.json records a hash per managed .bee/bin/** file, so re-syncing the mirror is not enough — self-onboard must re-run or ledger_parity --check stays red. Fix direction unchanged and still unshipped: derive all four regen/checks into the verify of any cell whose files hit templates/lib/, .bee/bin/lib/ or a rendered tree, so no author has to remember a count that has grown three times.

title: No mechanical guard that skills, references and the injected preamble stay migrated with the tree they describe
detail: okf-integration-close-f4 existed only because seven instruction surfaces still routed to the retired model while every content guard was green. Prose has no test, so it rots without a red, and the worst-hit surfaces are the ones review does not open: files a skill DEFERS to, and output generated into the session preamble. Mechanizable as a census suite: for a repo in bundle mode, fail when any file under skills/ or the injected preamble mentions the legacy state-layer path without also mentioning the bundle path. It would have caught all seven at authoring time and would keep catching them for every future migration.

title: route-live-state-untouched reads LIVE .bee/state.json, so the session running the chain can trip its own suite
detail: Observed twice this session and independently by a worker: node scripts/run_verify.mjs fails once on route-live-state-untouched, and an immediate re-run passes with no failure at all. Cause: the suite asserts against the live state file while this session's own Stop/UserPromptSubmit hooks mutate it mid-run. A suite whose subject is live mutable state cannot be run reliably from inside a live session. Impact is worse than a flake: it teaches everyone to re-run on red, which is exactly the habit that hides a real red. Fix direction: the suite should assert against a snapshot it copies first, or fixture its own state root, the way every other state suite already does.

title: test_store_lock case (c) flakes under the full chain at concurrency=5
README.md:271 says '6 lifecycle hooks' for Claude Code, which matches no measured quantity
The frozen judge reads rendered test projections as undeclared test-source edits
Force-unclaim + a concurrent merge silently destroyed a live worker's claim, reservations and uncommitted work (cz-5)
A live session force-unclaimed another live session's cell mid-flight and its merge destroyed 10 minutes of uncommitted work
Verify tiering: narrow per-cell verify (seconds) + full suite only at batch/slice boundaries, run in background
detail: User-settled direction 2026-07-23: stop running the full run_verify.mjs suite per change. Shape: (a) split commands.verify into quick (targeted, seconds) vs full (suite, boundaries only, background); (b) derive suite subset from changed files via static import graph (scope measured, not assumed); (c) red-proof gate for new tests (stash source diff, assert new test fails on old code); (d) optional: parallel test-writer/code-writer workers from one interface-bearing contract for behavior_change cells. 77 cells historically used full suite as cell verify; capCell never inspects the command, so narrow verify is already legal.

title: bee-write-guard enforces cross-worktree containment for Edit/Write but not for relative Bash targets
startFeature refuses to bootstrap a worktree that inherited its feature's open cells
No concept owns the dual-runtime enforcement doctrine, so the corrected statement has no durable home
No check refuses a recurrence appended to a pattern whose headline is byte-unchanged
A shape review can pass without a single command having been executed
write-guard denies writes to the harness-designated scratchpad, forcing agents to improvise temp storage
detail: The Claude Code harness assigns a per-session scratchpad under /tmp and instructs agents to use it for all temporary files. bee-write-guard denies any Bash command whose extracted targets fall outside the physical worktree, so every scratchpad write is refused as a traversal escape. Hit twice in one session: a sizes.txt measurement dump had to be rewritten as a single awk pipeline, and the same containment rule is why cross-worktree dispatch from a main-checkout session is impossible (a session must EnterWorktree to work a bee worktree, which the 'bee worktree new' output does not say). Impact: agents either lose the scratchpad affordance silently or, worse, read the guard as noise. Options: allowlist the session scratchpad root, or have the guard's denial name the scratchpad case explicitly so the fix is obvious.

title: After a legitimate feature close there is no phase from which source can ever be pushed
test_installers_e2e --installer bash flakes under run_verify concurrency
detail: During router-cost rc-4, the baseline run_verify went red on test_installers_e2e --installer bash, single test 'plugin transition occurs only after confirmation' — 'onboarding recheck expected up_to_date, got changes_needed'. Re-run in isolation: 23 passed, 0 failed. Passed in both subsequent full runs. Not caused by the cell. run_verify already has a serial sub-chain for _race/_lock/_concurrency-named suites plus test_heartbeat_touch (run_verify.mjs:347-355) and a serial live-bundle group (:387-395); this installer suite is in neither, but it mutates onboarding state that other suites read. Likely belongs in one of the serial groups. Impact: an intermittent red baseline teaches sessions to distrust the baseline gate, which is exactly the gate that must stay trustworthy — two workers in this feature already reported blockers that did not reproduce.

title: bee-model-guard denials recur: dispatch contract (tier marker vs model param) is trial-and-error, not pre-declared
detail: User friction 2026-07-23: a swarm dispatch carried [bee-tier: ceiling] + model:'opus' and was denied (ceiling resolves to no model name). This class of denial keeps recurring because the tier->model table and the marker-vs-param rule live in routing-and-contracts.md prose; agents improvise a combination and only learn the rule from the hook's FIX text. User asks: every naming/dispatch rule should be explicitly specified at the point of dispatch (e.g. preamble digest, claim-next output, or hook allowlist echo) so the first attempt is formed correctly instead of guard-corrected.

title: write-guard denies the Claude harness's own out-of-repo storage (memory dir, scratchpad)
detail: 2026-07-23: Write to /home/thanhsmind/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/*.md and Bash heredoc to the session scratchpad (/tmp/claude-*/scratchpad) were both denied with 'could not be canonically contained inside the physical worktree' — even after ExitWorktree returned the session to the main checkout. These are harness-provisioned dirs (auto-memory, scratchpad), not repo source; the guard should allowlist them (or scope containment checks to repo-relative targets only). Cost today: a user-preference memory could not be persisted; cell JSON had to route through stdin instead of scratchpad.

Proposal: session-close (and/or chain-nudge) compares commits touching tracked files since session start against decisions.jsonl/capture-queue.jsonl entries from the same session; zero entries plus at least one commit => loud what-settled-here nudge naming the commits. Prose tier ships in capture-invariant civ-1; this PBI is the enforcement tier.

title: The product backlog is per-item files with a generated index, so concurrent sessions never contend on one markdown table and a session reads only the rows it needs

title: Every bee skill reads and writes the knowledge bundle instead of loose markdown
cos: bee-scribing writes concepts, bee-compounding promotes them, bee-hive assembles its context packet from `knowledge context`, bee-grooming hunts stale knowledge — and Claude, Codex and any other runtime consume one shared bundle through per-runtime adapters, so a handover needs only the Markdown bundle, not the bee runtime (deferred by okf-foundation D22; requires P65). **Delivered per clause** — *scribing writes concepts*: okf-switchover-f3 f3-2/f3-3, one bundle-mode predicate + three-layer anti-fork gate. *bee-hive assembles from knowledge context*: f3-1/f3-4 (relevance-ranked criticals, bundle-first reading order) and okf-integration-close-f4 f4-1/f4-3 (the two reference files it defers to, and the session preamble itself, now bundle-routed). *grooming hunts stale knowledge*: f4-2 — its debt hunt counts the conformance check's own findings plus areas with code and no concept. **Remaining** — *compounding promotes them*: the `knowledge promote` verb exists but bee-compounding does not call it; promotion is still hand-authored. *per-runtime adapters*: a bundle-only handover (no bee runtime) is unstarted and unproven.

title: The ~65% context handoff rule is measurable, then enforceable
cos: Today AGENTS.md critical rule 6 is prose only — nothing reads context usage and nothing writes HANDOFF.json at a threshold. The compaction counter from P71 is the first measurement that makes the rule checkable; this row is the rule itself (a threshold, a trigger, and a test that proves it fires)

title: Codex hook execution is a proven fact, not an open question
cos: A probe that establishes whether an installed Codex CLI actually discovers and executes `.codex/hooks.json`, with a recorded result per codex-cli version. This is the unknown that forces the helper-floor constraint (compaction-hardening D3) and the hedge in the doctrine text (D2); resolving it lets a later feature state hook parity outright

title: The behaviour corpus is language-neutral, so any implementation can be validated by the same tests
cos: **Phase 0 of the Rust migration (decision `51d54157`, [strategy](decisions/0025-rust-migration-strategy.md)) and the gate on every later phase.** 35 of 56 suites already drive the CLI across a process boundary and validate any implementation as-is; the other 18 import JS modules directly and are the only structural lock-in. CoS: (1) those 18 suites are expressed as data — `(input, repo state) → expected verdict/output` — executed against whatever binary is under test; (2) no expectation encodes a JS internal (a module name, an export, an error class); (3) the full corpus runs green against today's JS runtime; (4) a deliberately broken build fails it (the corpus must bite). Valuable even if the migration is abandoned

title: Rust write-guard hook as a deferring fast path
cos: **Phase 1.** Scoped in [CONTEXT](history/rust-write-guard/CONTEXT.md) (D1-D5). CoS: hook ≤5ms measured by the 10-run median method **against the import-trimmed JS baseline (~25ms), not today's 42ms**; the P60 corpus green against the binary; anything the binary does not fully recognize defers to JS; absent/incompatible binary ⇒ JS runs unchanged. Stop-condition from the strategy: if the real-session win is under ~15ms/call, the migration stops after hooks

title: Binaries ship reproducibly for five targets before the second port
cos: **Phase 2 — the real cost of the migration, and it must exist before porting beyond the first hook.** bee ships today by copying files with no build step anywhere. CoS: CI cross-compiles linux x64/arm64, macOS x64/arm64, windows x64; checksums published and verified at install; the installer selects by platform and silently falls back to JS on no match; a tagged release proves all five plus the fallback

title: The runtime is Rust, one process at a time, JS deleted as it goes
cos: **Phases 3-5**, in this order: remaining hooks → read-only verbs (status/list/show/search) → single-writer mutators → **concurrency layer last** (lock/claims/reservations/worktree-holds — three genuine correctness bugs surfaced there in one day, and its cost is I/O, not CPU). CoS per unit: corpus green, the unit's JS **deleted in the same feature** (no dual implementations — every drift incident this repo has had was two copies of one truth), store format unchanged in that step, and the phase independently revertible by deleting a binary

title: CLI reads land under 100ms — lazy module loading first, a Rust write-guard hook binary second
cos: Trigger-conditioned (decision `2e224c90`, user follow-up 2026-07-21): (1) lazy-load the dispatcher so each verb imports only its own module slice — target simple reads ~30ms, status ~200ms; (2) if per-tool-call hook tax still hurts after that, port ONLY the write-guard hook to a small Rust binary (48-53ms → ~3ms per tool call), keeping node orchestration + copy-in distribution; full-CLI rust rewrite stays rejected while remaining cost is OS-bound I/O+git. Prove with the same 5-run median methodology as cli-performance

title: Write-time deny gains a wait/queue option
cos: Today a file conflict is a hard deny and a `[BLOCKED]` worker idles until orchestrator rescue. Once computed waves (P39) land, measure whether real stalls remain; only then consider a bounded wait/queue primitive at the write guard (deferred from parallel-scheduler exploring D4, 2026-07-15)

title: Installer offers the status line as a one-flag opt-in
cos: A real user installed bee and reported "I don't see the status line" — it is deliberately opt-in (decision 102efe08: onboarding never touches settings.json uninvited) and today enabling it is a manual 2-file copy + settings edit from `.claude/skills/bee-hive/templates/statusline/`. Add `--statusline` to install.sh/install.ps1 (and an onboard flag): with explicit consent it copies the pair to `~/.claude/` (or repo-level with the repo-scoped command) and merges the `statusLine` entry into settings.json with a `.bak`, same merge discipline as the hooks merge. Give records a subject/predicate/value claim. Needs its own exploring: it is a data-model change to an append-only log with 135 existing events (schema, migration, CLI shape, compatibility with `activeDecisions`/`supersede`) (owner decision 2026-07-14)

title: A crashed session's unsettled context is recovered from the harness transcript

Teach recovery to mine the harness-persisted session transcript (~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl) as a SECONDARY reconciliation source against decisions/state — decisions stay primary memory, raw transcripts are mined by a down-tier worker and never loaded as session context (source: user field report 2026-07-20; friction row filed same day). SHIPPED 2026-07-20: cells transcript-recovery-1..4 — recovery.mjs (detection + bounded mining window + down-tier miner prompt), `recovery scan|window` CLI + fail-open status block, `capture add --source mined` for unconfirmed mined settlements, test_recovery wired into the verify chain + bee-hive Session Scout offer paragraph; all 4 judged PASS, full verify green ([docs/history/transcript-recovery/](history/transcript-recovery/))

title: Bee knows when to ask a second opinion, and stops doing the work itself

Origin: a real host session billed $24 on ceiling against $0.41 across three subagents — under-delegation, not over-reading; the first diagnosis was overturned by an adversarial cross-family advisor, which is the feature's own argument for existing (owner conversation 2026-07-14; decisions D1–D6) (status decoration: ([docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/](../docs/history/advisor-and-orchestration/)))

title: A cell cannot be authored without the regen obligations its own file list implies
cos: `cells add` DERIVES the derived-artifact obligation from the cell's `files`: any path under `skills/**` or `hooks/**` (what `scripts/release_manifest.mjs:131,133` actually hashes) requires `release_manifest.mjs --check` in that cell's verify and the manifest path in its files; any path under `templates/lib/` or `.bee/bin/lib/` additionally requires `ledger_parity.mjs --check`. Today's `manifestLintWarning` (`bee.mjs:817`) asks the wrong question — it fires only when the verify ALREADY mentions `release_manifest`, so omitting the check entirely dodges it. PAT37 has recurred 5 times and named mechanization as the fix direction after the 2nd; compaction-hardening then failed two consecutive shape reviews on exactly this, both times because the scope was set from assumption rather than measured **Delivered** — ro-1 (commit `e4ae329`): `cells add`/`cells update` refuse a cell whose `files` touch a hashed root unless its own `verify` carries that guard's `--check`. Roots DERIVED from the scripts at every write, never hard-coded — the derivation immediately measured 12 manifest roots where every recited count said 1, 2 or 6, so a literal list would have shipped blind to five. Escape hatch `regen_obligation_ack` (reason string, not a boolean). Selftest exhibits the refusal on both grounds separately against synthetic guard scripts naming roots bee has never heard of, so a row can only pass by actually reading the script. Red evidence is a mutation of the guard into the exact defect. History: docs/knowledge/patterns/20260715-shipping-a-lib-file-means-shipping-the-manifest.md

title: A session that gets compacted lands back on the work it was doing
cos: The intent anchor is prompted, never merely available (a nudge fires when work is active and no anchor exists); every compaction is recorded to `.bee/logs/compaction.jsonl` with a per-session and per-cell survival count; `SessionStart(source=compact)` gets a compact-scoped capsule instead of the full startup preamble while every other source stays byte-identical; a read-only `state compact-check` sweep reports lane/claim/gate/dependency/hold mismatches and never repairs; and the doctrine text stops telling every session that Codex enforcement is prose-only. Every behavior reachable by a bee.mjs verb, hooks as fast path only (compaction-hardening D3) **Delivered per clause** — *anchor prompted*: cz-7 (nudge on UserPromptSubmit deduped + PreCompact forced; it fired unprompted in the closing session). *every compaction recorded with per-session and per-cell counts*: cz-3 (`compaction.mjs` append + counting, resume never re-increments) + cz-7 (PreCompact wiring). *capsule on compact, every other source byte-identical*: cz-5 (builder + committed golden) + cz-6 (SessionStart branch; `test_hook_contracts.mjs` green with zero edits). *read-only sweep that never repairs*: cz-3 (`compactCheck`, idempotence proven by tree hash) + cz-4 (verb). *doctrine stops misinforming*: cz-1 (twelve live corrections) + cz-2 (derived gate knowing four hook counts apart). *every behavior reachable by a verb*: cz-4 (`compact-log`, `compact-check`) + cz-5 (`compact-capsule`). All six clauses carry evidence; 89/89 suites green. History: docs/history/compaction-hardening/

title: Git/VCS commands are exempt from the intake gate
cos: A `git commit`/`git status` between features is bookkeeping, not source work — today the intake gate blocks git once phase is terminal, forcing workarounds. Decide the exemption's exact shape (read-only always allowed? commit allowed when staged paths are docs/.bee only?) and implement it in the write guard without reopening the door the gate exists to shut (deferred from GitHub #1, reporter discussion open since v1.3.10; registered here 2026-07-17 so it stops living only in a closed-issue thread). Per-clause evidence at flip (D11b): (1) shape decided — delivered, [CONTEXT.md](history/intake-gate-git-exemption/CONTEXT.md) D1/D3/D4 (read-only git always allowed, deliberately enumerated with fail-closed on an unrecognized subcommand; a mutating git command is exempt at a terminal phase only when every path it would actually change is bookkeeping — `.bee/`, `docs/`, `plans/`, `AGENTS.md`; `git push` never exempt; eligibility never inferred from a flag or commit message); (2) implemented in the write guard — delivered, `checkGitBashCommand` in `skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/guards.mjs` (mirrored to `.bee/bin/lib/guards.mjs`), wired into `hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs`, resolves real staged/target paths via `git diff --cached --name-only` at check time (D4); (3) door not reopened — delivered, scoped to terminal phases only (a staged source path outside a terminal phase is untouched by this logic), an unrecognized git subcommand fails closed, and a misleading `-m` message changes nothing. Proof: `hooks/test_write_guard.mjs` rows 47-56, full suite 147/147 green

title: A reversed decision propagates to every artifact that cites it, a backlog row only flips done when its CoS is actually met, and decisions stay findable at scale
cos: (1) `decisions supersede` runs a propagation sweep over `docs/**` for the superseded id and refuses to finish silently — every hit is reconciled same-turn or becomes a capture stub; (2) done-flip enumerates the row's CoS clauses with cited evidence and never flips on a delivered subset (row keeps Delivered/Remaining annotation instead); (3) decide events accept `tags[]`/`area`, search gains `--tag/--area/--since/--all`, a CLI-rendered index lands under `docs/decisions/`, and an archive verb keeps the active store small — all proven by tests + one end-to-end supersede dry-run (GH #32/#33/#34). title: readBacklogCounts silently drops 'done ([link](path))' rows from all counts/rank/badges
detail: normalizeStatus in lib/backlog.mjs requires an EXACT match to 'done'/'proposed'/'in-flight' after stripping *`_ markup. The established convention of appending a docs/history link after done -- 'done ([docs/history/<feature>/](history/<feature>/))', seen in P77, P32, and now P79 -- leaves the status token as 'done (...)' which BACKLOG_STATUSES.includes() rejects. These rows are excluded from readBacklogCounts entirely (not counted as done, not counted as anything), and the rank pass falls back to RANK_UNKNOWN_WEIGHT for them. README badges therefore under-report the true done count. Either normalizeStatus should tolerate a trailing annotation after the bare status word, or the done-row link convention needs a different home (e.g. Feature column) that does not collide with the Status column's exact-match parse.

title: Wire a real human-queue-submit caller for bee backlog add --queue-submit
detail: backlog-auto-commit-2 (P79) narrowed commitBacklogRow to only auto-commit when --queue-submit is true, default false. Inventory of the 17 existing bee backlog add call sites across 9 skills files found ALL of them are agent self-observation (friction/debt/finding) -- correctly none pass --queue-submit. Net effect: P78's auto-commit capability is currently dormant -- no live code path in the repo triggers a commit today. Needs a genuine human-explicit-queue-submission call site (e.g. when a human directly asks the agent to log something into .bee/backlog.jsonl for later processing) identified or created and wired to pass --queue-submit, or the capability stays permanently inert.

title: Tighten bee-compounding's promotion decision tree: a later feature citing an existing friction entry is not a 'seen twice' recurrence
detail: backlog-submit-command's close (2026-07-23) nearly mis-scored the readBacklogCounts silent-drop bug as a second sighting because its own scribing pass cited the original 2026-07-23T10:20:34.557Z friction entry in a new concept doc. Failure analyst caught it: citing an existing finding is not an independent discovery/friction event. Suggest bee-compounding's SKILL.md step 4 (Promote Criticals) explicitly state that only a freshly-filed friction/blocked-work record counts toward 'seen twice' -- a later feature's doc merely referencing an earlier finding by timestamp does not.

title: Codify a standing plan-checker rule: a cell adding a command-registry.mjs entry must declare its registry-coverage test file up front
detail: backlog-submit-command-1's worker had to touch test_bee_cli.mjs (undeclared in the cell's files) because run_verify.mjs's pre-existing 'every registry entry had its example executed at least once' guard failed once command-registry.mjs gained the new backlog.propose entry. This is the same class of gap as the already-codified templates/lib-touch-requires-release-manifest-regen rule (decision 2412ccdc, 2026-07-15) -- currently uncodified for the registry-coverage guard specifically. Suggest bee-planning's cells add validation (or bee-validating's plan-checker) flag any cell whose files touch lib/command-registry.mjs but omit a test file exercising the new entry's example.
